studyName: Changes in Oral and Gut Microbiota and Incidence and Severity of Patient-Reported Symptoms in Pre- and Post-Kidney Transplant Patients

tables.description: This dataset contains the raw data from the Kidney Disease Quality of Life Short Form (KDQOL-SF). (See KDQoL info for data labels): The KDQoL-SF is a measure kidney disease specific Health related quality of life (HRQOL) questionnaire. The core of the KDQOL-SF includes the generic SF-36 health survey used in the Medical Outcomes Study.107 The SF-36 is a psychometrically based generic HRQOL instrument consisting of eight subscales (Physical Functioning, Role-Physical, Bodily Pain, General Health, Vitality, Social Functioning, Role-Emotional, and Mental Health). The eight subscales result in two distinct composite scores: the Physical Composite Score (PCS) and Mental Composite Score (MCS).108 In addition to the core measures, the KDQOL-SF includes 43 kidney disease-specific items divided into domains, including symptoms/problems, effects of kidney disease, burden of kidney disease, work status, cognitive function, quality of social interaction, sexual function, sleep, social support, staff encouragement, and patient satisfaction.71 Higher scores indicate a higher level of HRQOL. In previous studies, internal consistency reliability estimates were greater than 0.80 for kidney disease items (except cognitive function = 0.68 and quality of social interaction = 0.61), and between 0.78 and 0.92 for the items of the 36-item core.

This dataset includes standardized scores that range from 0-100 (t-score with SD of 10), with 0 indicating worst possible HRQOL score and 100 indicating best possible. Scoring templates and other information can be found at Rand: https://www.rand.org/healthcare/surveys_tools/kdqol.html

This dataset contains the raw scores from the PROMIS 57 instrument. Patient Reported Outcomes Measurement System (PROMIS) 57 Profile v2.1. (see PROMIS info for data labels): contains a fixed number of items from seven PROMIS domains (Depression, Anxiety, Physical Function, Pain Interference, Fatigue, Sleep Disturbance, and Ability to Participate in Social Roles and Activities). The PROMIS-57 has 8 questions per domain (rate 1 to 5-raw scores for each domain range from 4-40) and includes an additional pain intensity 0-10 numeric rating scale. The profiles are universal rather than disease-specific. They assess all domains over the past 7 days except for Physical Function, which has no timeframe specified.105. The PROMIS 57v2.1 has been validated for use in kidney transplant recipient.

tables.description: This dataset contains the transformed raw scores from the PROMIS 57 instrument. Scores are transformed using the HealthMeasures website.  Raw scores are converted to t-scores (0-100 with a standard deviation of 10).  Lower scores indicate less of the concept, while higher scores indicate worse symptoms (e.gh., higher pain).  Patient Reported Outcomes Measurement System (PROMIS) 57 Profile v2.1. (see PROMIS info for data labels): contains a fixed number of items from seven PROMIS domains (Depression, Anxiety, Physical Function, Pain Interference, Fatigue, Sleep Disturbance, and Ability to Participate in Social Roles and Activities). The PROMIS-57 has 8 questions per domain (includes an additional pain intensity 0-10 numeric rating scale. The profiles are universal rather than disease-specific. They assess all domains over the past 7 days except for Physical Function, which has no timeframe specified.105. The PROMIS 57v2.1 has been validated for use in kidney transplant recipient.

variables.name: ItmCnt
variables.description: Number of items on the PROMIS instrument

variables.name: RawScore
variables.description: Raw PROMIS score by subcategory (physical function, anxiety, depression, fatigue, sleep disturbance, ability to participate in social roles, pain interference, pain intensity)

variables.name: ScrdCnt
variables.description: Number of domains used to score

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.
